features.name: Rage
features.description: On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action. While raging, you gain the following benefits: Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra 2d6 damage on a hit. You have advantage on Strength saving throws and on attack rolls using strength-based ability scores. You can't cast spells, and you lose this ability while under the effects of a spell. If you end a turn with 0 hit points or if your rage ends, you must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw, taking 2d6 poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you are wearing no armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier.

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.description: On your turn, you can take the Reckless Attack action on your attack roll, which lets you add +2 to the roll. However, until the end of your next turn, you take a -2 penalty to AC.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would knock you prone.

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the save, and only half damage if you fail.

features.name: Feral Instinct
features.description: You gain proficiency in Perception and Acrobatics.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can impose disadvantage on an attack roll made against you if it's based on Dexterity, Strength, or Constitution.

features.name: Natural Resistance
features.description: You gain resistance to one damage type of your choice: bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing.

features.name: Rage Competence
features.description: You can use the Attack action and deal an extra 2d6 damage on a hit. You can do so once per turn.

features.name: Feral Transformation
features.description: On your turn when you have at least one rage left, you can assume a feline form for up to 1 hour or until you are incapacitated.
